measurement: branch fork, merge, discard, promote, and later evidence access rates
expected_signal: survival and post-merge reuse distribution
support_condition: Option C branch-retention value is positive
falsification_condition: branches die without later evidence access

measurement: verifier-state access across candidates, retries, regressions, and audits
expected_signal: verifier reuse and avoided repeated validation
support_condition: verifier state is a reusable correctness-sensitive object
falsification_condition: verifier state is single-use or not replay-authorized

measurement: replay/citation of previous tool outputs versus rerun
expected_signal: replay avoids tool reruns or preserves evidence
support_condition: tool outputs carry retained value beyond current prompt
falsification_condition: tool outputs are never reused or cheap to rerun

measurement: workspace artifacts used as future dependencies
expected_signal: cross-session durable dependency graph
support_condition: durable workspace supports Option C
falsification_condition: durable artifacts are archival only and not execution inputs

measurement: reuse-distance distribution for trajectory-scoped objects
expected_signal: long-tail reuse beyond request-local scope
support_condition: agentic memory exceeds token/KV lifetime
falsification_condition: reuse distances are short and KV-like

workload_target: production/open traces replayed through runtime prototype
measurement: architecture decision with fields hidden
required_instrumentation: all DC-005 fields plus runtime option computation
expected_signal: hiding branch/verifier/durable fields collapses C to B/A
support_condition: M-PROTO-1 causal boundary generalizes
falsification_condition: architecture choice unchanged by hiding fields

measurement: false-positive trajectory reuse check
required_instrumentation: same trace parser as agentic workloads
expected_signal: zero/inert DAG fields and no Option C recommendation
support_condition: measurement discriminates controls
falsification_condition: controls show spurious Option C from logging artifacts

measurement: raw semantic hit rate versus valid-hit rate
required_instrumentation: cache_key_hash; semantic_match_score; correctness_label; provenance_id; source_version; invalidation_signal
expected_signal: valid hits are lower than raw hits by measurable false-positive and freshness losses
support_condition: Option B retained value uses P_hit * P_valid_given_hit rather than raw P_hit
falsification_condition: raw hits collapse after correctness and freshness validation

measurement: semantic false-positive rate by match-score bucket
expected_signal: false positives concentrate near marginal semantic match scores
support_condition: semantic-cache policy can set a safe operating threshold
falsification_condition: false-positive loss exceeds recompute avoided at useful hit thresholds

measurement: stale-hit rate after source version and invalidation changes
required_instrumentation: cache_key_hash; source_version; invalidation_signal; correctness_label; recovery_action; recovery_latency_ms
expected_signal: stale hits are rejected or recovered before reuse
support_condition: freshness and invalidation gates preserve Option B value
falsification_condition: stale hits pass or recovery exceeds recompute

measurement: safe hit rate after tenant scope and cache salt enforcement
expected_signal: tenant and salt gates reduce unsafe cross-boundary hits to zero
support_condition: Option B reuse remains positive after isolation gates
falsification_condition: isolation gates eliminate useful hits or unsafe hits pass

measurement: recovery latency and action after invalid cache hit
expected_signal: recovery cost is bounded and lower than recompute for useful entries
support_condition: invalid hits can fail closed without erasing retained value
falsification_condition: lookup plus validation plus recovery exceeds recompute

measurement: poisoning or untrusted-provenance rejection rate
expected_signal: untrusted or poisoned entries are rejected before reuse
support_condition: correctness-sensitive semantic reuse is provenance-safe
falsification_condition: untrusted provenance passes in correctness-sensitive paths

measurement: durable workspace read and write p50 p95 p99 by size and consistency mode
required_instrumentation: durable_object_id; object_class; size_bytes; consistency_mode; storage_tier; write_latency_ms; read_latency_ms
expected_signal: tail latency distribution is measurable per object class and consistency mode
support_condition: durable replay value uses p50 p95 p99 rather than median only
falsification_condition: p95 or p99 exceeds recompute avoided for latency-sensitive paths

measurement: dependency-path max replay latency under fan-in
expected_signal: fan-in path latency exposes tail amplification
support_condition: Option C durable replay remains useful after dependency-width tails
falsification_condition: fan-in max path latency erases retained value margin

measurement: summary-pointer recovery latency and validity
expected_signal: valid pointers reconstruct faster than recompute
support_condition: summary-plus-pointer replay preserves correctness-sensitive value
falsification_condition: invalid pointer or reconstruction p95 exceeds recompute p95

measurement: retention horizon and hold-state violations
expected_signal: expired or unauthorized durable replay is blocked
support_condition: retention gates make durable replay legally and operationally safe
falsification_condition: expired retention without active hold is replayed or credited

measurement: replay success rate under object-store tail injection
required_instrumentation: durable_object_id; dependency_path_id; dependency_width; read_latency_ms; consistency_wait_ms; pointer_valid; reconstruction_latency_ms
expected_signal: Option C decision flips when injected tails exceed retained value
support_condition: durable replay support is robust only below measured p95 p99 deadlines
falsification_condition: tail injection causes missed verifier or merge deadlines

workload_target: RAG; code-agent loop; verification-heavy; multi-agent branch/merge
measurement: per-gate provenance validation latency, CPU time, lookup count, and blocked-reuse rate
required_instrumentation: provenance_id; source_version; invalidation_signal; tenant_scope; cache_salt; actor_id; replay_authorization_scope; verifier_evidence_hash; retention_hold_state; validation_start_time; validation_end_time; validation_gate_ids
expected_signal: PV-01 through PV-08 reject invalid fixtures and stay below retained-value margins
support_condition: safe object and replay validation can run before retained value is credited
falsification_condition: any invalid fixture passes or p95 common-gate overhead exceeds retained-value margin

workload_target: RAG; code-agent loop; verification-heavy; multi-agent branch/merge
measurement: end-to-end trace replay overhead with provenance validation enabled

expected_signal: architecture decisions are recomputed after measured validation overhead is subtracted
support_condition: Option B/C decisions survive safe validation

measurement: safe hit rate and validation overhead after tenant/cache-salt partitioning
required_instrumentation: tenant_scope; cache_salt; cache_key_hash; validation_start_time; validation_end_time; validation_decision
expected_signal: safe hit rate remains positive after isolation gates

expected_signal: lineage and authorization overhead stays below branch/verifier/durable retained value
support_condition: Option C replay can be authorized with bounded overhead

measurement: verifier evidence hash binding and tamper rejection cost
expected_signal: tampered verifier evidence is rejected with bounded hash/lookup cost
support_condition: verifier-state reuse remains valid after integrity checks

measurement: retention hold and durable replay compliance validation cost
expected_signal: expired or unauthorized durable replay is rejected before value credit
support_condition: durable replay remains useful after retention checks
falsification_condition: expired reuse passes or retention checks add tail latency above recompute

measurement: summary-plus-pointer recoverability validation cost
required_instrumentation: compression_strategy; provenance_id; source_version; pointer_valid; reconstruction_latency_ms; validation_decision
expected_signal: pointer-preserving compression counts only when exact recovery is valid
support_condition: summary-plus-pointer remains safe and useful after provenance checks
